sameConditions: Run from the repository root on the current checkout.
Use Windows-safe npm.cmd and npx.cmd entrypoints.
Use deterministic local fixtures and the Playwright webServer defined in playwright.config.ts.
Do not update snapshots or regenerate catalog data during scenario validation.
Record command exit code, duration, selected tier, and bounded stdout/stderr evidence for every selected check.
passCriteria: The scenario catalog validates against every top-level QC registry surface.
Known skips or non-BattleMech exclusions are represented in the scenario notes instead of silently hiding failures.
After a fix, rerun the failed scenario first, then rerun the complete selected scenario set.
rerunPolicy: Fix the underlying cause, not the scenario definition, when a required check fails.
Rerun the affected scenario with the same tier and filters used for the failing run.
Rerun the complete selected scenario set before claiming the quality bar is met.

tiers.core: Fastest deterministic command(s) that prove the capability contract is still wired.
tiers.standard: Focused realistic flow proof, including browser coverage where the capability is user-facing.
tiers.extended: Heavier parity or broad-flow checks that are useful before release but not default for quick section QC.
tiers.diagnostic: Non-gating inventory or triage output captured for context.

scenarios.title: App shell navigation and installable routes remain usable
scenarios.realisticFlow: A player opens the app, moves across core routes, uses mobile navigation, and verifies the PWA shell remains reachable without dead ends.
successCriteria: Core routes render without unexpected client errors.
Navigation works in desktop and mobile shell contexts.
PWA affordances remain detectable and do not block normal app use.

checks.command: npx.cmd playwright test e2e/app-routes.spec.ts e2e/mobile-navigation.spec.ts e2e/pwa.spec.ts --project=chromium

notes: Full Chromium E2E remains available through npm.cmd run test:e2e; this scenario uses the focused route, mobile, and PWA shell specs plus the strict app-shell route/deep-link/refresh proof.

scenarios.title: Compendium search and official unit data stay catalog-backed
scenarios.realisticFlow: A player browses unit and equipment reference data while validators prove the official asset and generated schema contract is current.
successCriteria: Strict asset validation passes against the checked-in catalog inputs.
Generated schema output is current.
Compendium browser flows can search, filter, and open reference pages.
notes: Non-BattleMech system parity is intentionally split into separate validator matrices where BattleMech combat validation excludes it.

scenarios.title: Customizer construction, BV validation, and export flows agree
scenarios.realisticFlow: A player creates supported units in the customizer while validators prove BV and construction math are sourced from current reference data.
successCriteria: BattleMech, vehicle, aerospace, battle armor, ProtoMech, and infantry BV validators pass.
Customizer UI flows for supported unit types load and allow creation/navigation.
Exotic unit paths remain represented instead of silently falling back to stale data.

checks.command: npm.cmd run validate:vehicle && npm.cmd run validate:aerospace && npm.cmd run validate:ba-bv && npm.cmd run validate:proto && npm.cmd run validate:infantry

notes: BV reproducibility must remain tied to live reference assets rather than stale cached expectations.

scenarios.title: Force, pilot, and encounter setup reaches a playable launch
scenarios.realisticFlow: A player creates a force, builds an encounter, launches it, and the service layer converts the setup into a game session.
successCriteria: Force and encounter browser flows can create and launch setup data.
Encounter services persist, hydrate, and convert encounters into sessions.
Invalid setup references are rejected or surfaced before gameplay commitment.
notes: The previous permissive encounter-flow smoke is historical context only; strict continuity must launch a campaign encounter, reach combat, queue post-battle outcome state, reload review, and apply the outcome.

scenarios.title: Tactical map previews explain the same actions the engine allows
scenarios.realisticFlow: A player inspects terrain, movement, LOS, and combat previews in top-down and isometric map modes before committing actions.
successCriteria: Movement and combat scenario fixtures agree with engine validation.
Invalid movement or attack options expose reasons before commitment.
Top-down terrain/elevation labels, overlays, isometric rotation, occlusion, and action menu paths remain visible in browser smoke coverage.
notes: Legacy preview fallback paths, physical command availability, and phase-driver agreement remain high-risk checks inside this scenario.

scenarios.title: Simulation combat validation stays catalog-driven and gap-honest
scenarios.realisticFlow: A combat sequence validates official catalog-backed behavior, gap accounting, and runner/interactive agreement for movement and attacks.
successCriteria: validate:combat passes without static fallback data hiding catalog failures.
BattleMech combat gap summary remains explicit and out-of-scope rows stay separated.
Interactive movement and attack projection agreement scenario tests pass.
notes: Known non-BattleMech scope splits are not scenario failures when the gap summary reports them separately.

scenarios.title: Campaign economy, repair, salvage, and progression loops survive a battle
scenarios.realisticFlow: A player creates a campaign, carries damage forward, reviews battle outcomes, and uses repair/progression systems.
successCriteria: Campaign flow and round-trip browser specs pass.
Campaign dashboard operations queue exposes unresolved battle, salvage, repair, medical, finance, contract, and GM audit handoffs.
Campaign economy browser coverage proves dashboard, repair, salvage, finances, and day advance after post-battle state reloads.
Campaign outcome, bay, acquisition, market, salvage, and repair processor unit tests preserve post-battle state transitions.
notes: The broad npm.cmd test -- --runInBand lane is intentionally replaced here by focused campaign economy contracts and a strict Chromium browser flow.

scenarios.title: Multiplayer and co-op sync preserve match visibility and route continuity
scenarios.realisticFlow: A host and players exercise multiplayer route smoke, P2P harness behavior, fog visibility, and reconnection contracts.
successCriteria: P2P harness and multiplayer playtest route smoke specs pass.
Fog-of-war match API tests preserve visibility boundaries.
Server reconnection, spectate, turn authority, and capstone host/join/recovery tests preserve co-op state assumptions.
notes: `verify:qc:multiplayer-reliability` runs the browser route harness plus these server/API contracts as one on-demand gate.
True multi-peer BroadcastChannel sharing remains skipped in Playwright; this scenario records route, mock harness, fog, reconnect, spectate, turn-authority, and server contract coverage instead.

scenarios.title: Replay and audit history remain deterministic and inspectable
scenarios.realisticFlow: A player opens replay/audit views while simulation replay determinism proves stored events reconstruct the same terminal state.
successCriteria: Replay player and simulation viewer browser flows pass.
Event log replay determinism passes.
Replay-library watch/back navigation, uploaded replay playback, and keyboard shortcuts pass without relying on skipped browser checks.
Timeline export and data-dependent campaign/completed-game replay entry checks remain visible as release-signoff gaps instead of being counted as proof.
notes: Replay Library watch/back and upload keyboard flows are browser-proved in `e2e/replay-player.spec.ts`; timeline export plus data-dependent campaign/completed-game entry checks stay tracked in the replay surface gap.

scenarios.title: Desktop packaging, local services, and API security boundaries hold
scenarios.realisticFlow: A desktop build check runs while local desktop services and vault API tests prove persisted settings, recent files, and sensitive API routes behave within their boundaries.
successCriteria: Desktop build test passes for the packaged runtime assumptions.
Desktop local settings and recent-file services pass focused tests.
Vault API tests preserve identity, unlock, signing, and sharing boundaries.

checks.command: npx.cmd jest --selectProjects unit --ci --runTestsByPath src/__tests__/api/vault/vault.test.ts src/__tests__/api/vault/unlock.test.ts src/__tests__/api/vault/sign.test.ts src/__tests__/api/vault/share.test.ts src/__tests__/api/vault/identity.test.ts --no-coverage

notes: Artifact audit checks app.asar window hardening, IPC path sandboxing, CSP headers, loopback server payload, and Electron-ABI native module placement.

scenarios.title: OpenSpec, CI, and quality gates keep claims reviewable
scenarios.realisticFlow: A maintainer validates the spec tree, registry references, type contracts, and formatting/lint gates before treating a quality claim as release-ready.
successCriteria: OpenSpec strict validation passes.
QC registry validation passes.
Typecheck, lint, and format checks pass for the current checkout.

checks.command: npm.cmd run typecheck -- --pretty false && npm.cmd run lint && npm.cmd run format:check

notes: This is a QC capability scenario, not a user gameplay flow; it proves claims and registry references stay reviewable.

scenarios.title: Maintenance code-health gates stay triageable on demand
scenarios.realisticFlow: A maintainer validates that the maintenance scanner has not regressed past baseline, that Wave 12 repo-wide findings are ledger-accounted, and that partial QC lanes remain available for the next cleanup wave.
successCriteria: Maintenance scan gate passes against docs/qc/maintenance-baseline.json.
The maintenance warning ledger accounts for every actionable repo-wide Wave 12 finding as fixed, accepted, or follow-up.
QC partial-lane selection remains available for planning the next remediation wave.
Raw maintenance inventory can be captured as diagnostic evidence without being mistaken for a pass/fail release gate.
notes: Passing the baseline gate proves the src regression gate; the warning ledger validator proves Wave 12 repo-wide accounting.
